pages.full_text:
We arrived at __Ste Marie sur l'Ouche__ &
hung the washing & it dried. The weather is
now warmer & it's not threatening rain.
~~Saturday~~ Sunday
We cycled 27 miles, along the Canal de
Bourgogne away from Dijon to Eclus
Pont de Pany. We had lunch at Bryony's.
An amazing set-up next to the canal by
an English woman who spoke fluent French.
The food was just what we needed, but as
Charles said - English & not good. Simon had
ham & chips, Eve fish fingers & chips, Jenny
chicken nuggets & chips & Charles quiche &
chips - Not a tomato or green veg in sight!
The coffee was good. Jenny had forgotten her
sheepskin saddle cover & had a sore bum.
Eve had a sore bum even with the cover.
Limericks at Supper.

Monday
We set off before Jenny & Charles. They maybe
going to visit Paia. We went towards Sens
for a supermarket & the on to Provins.
pages.notes: Page is a continuation from the previous spread — top line begins mid-sentence. 'Saturday' is crossed out and 'Sunday' written above. 'Bryony's' is the name of a canal-side restaurant run by an English woman; spelling uncertain, could be 'Bryony's' or similar. 'Eclus Pont de Pany' — 'Eclus' likely refers to 'écluse' (lock) on the canal; 'Pont de Pany' is a place near Dijon on the Canal de Bourgogne. 'Paia' is uncertain — could be a place name or a person's name. No year visible on page; cannot anchor year from content alone.

pages.full_text:
We cycled past lots of Ecluses, lock-keepers houses 40[illegible]

[Photo: postcard glued in, captioned "En Bourgogne" — showing a lock-keeper's house beside a canal with mist on the water]

We reached an unusual campsite, a bit like
Center Parks, around gravel pits, Hermes Loisirs
also 'La Fontaine' [illegible] at Gouaix.
There's a bar & one of the pits is a
swimming pool. After tea we rode our
bikes around the site & had a drink at the [bar?]
pages.notes: Top of page is a continuation of text from left page — 'We cycled past lots of Ecluses, lock-keepers houses 40' then text is cut off at the scan edge. Large postcard of a canal lock-keeper's house in Burgundy is glued in, captioned 'En Bourgogne' in printed teal text. Below the photo, text mentions campsite 'Hermes Loisirs' also called 'La Fontaine' at Gouaix (a commune in Seine-et-Marne). Some words after '[illegible]' between 'La Fontaine' and 'at Gouaix' are unclear. Last line cut off at right edge — 'drink at the [bar?]' is my best reading. 'Center Parks' likely refers to Center Parcs holiday village concept. Ink is blue (different from left page which is black).
